pr.title: feat: add permissionMode control (approveAll, denyShell, readOnly)
pr.author: Copilot
pr.description:
The node hardcoded `approveAll` for every session, giving the agent unrestricted access to shell, file, network, and all other operations. A `permissionMode` parameter in `Additional Fields` lets workflow authors enforce a safety policy without touching the SDK's permission callback API.

## Changes

### `session.ts`
- Exports `PermissionMode` type: `'approveAll' | 'denyShell' | 'readOnly'`
- Adds `buildPermissionHandler(mode)` factory:
  - `approveAll` → SDK's built-in `approveAll` (no behaviour change)
  - `denyShell` → denies `kind === 'shell'`, approves everything else
  - `readOnly` → approves only `kind === 'read'`, denies all other kinds
- `executeSharedSession` / `executeIsolatedSession` accept an optional `permissionMode` (default `'approveAll'`), passed to `createSession`

### `CopilotAgent.node.ts`
- Adds `Additional Fields` collection containing a `permissionMode` dropdown with per-option use-case descriptions
- Reads `permissionMode` from `additionalFields` (falls back to `'approveAll'`) and forwards it to the session functions

### `__tests__/session.test.ts` *(new)*
- 14 unit tests covering `buildPermissionHandler` across all three modes and all SDK `PermissionRequest.kind` values (`shell`, `read`, `write`, `mcp`, `url`, `custom-tool`)

## Permission handler

```typescript
export function buildPermissionHandler(mode: PermissionMode): PermissionHandler {
    if (mode === 'approveAll') return approveAll;
    if (mode === 'denyShell')
        return (req) =>
            req.kind === 'shell'
                ? { kind: 'denied-interactively-by-user' }
                : { kind: 'approved' };
    // readOnly
    return (req) =>
        req.kind === 'read' ? { kind: 'approved' } : { kind: 'denied-interactively-by-user' };
}
```

Denied permissions surface as errors in the item output (`success: false`) via the existing `catch` blocks in both session functions — no unhandled exceptions.
